payload_config.mock_overrides: only for intentional payload_validation test signals

H1.name: Full Happy Path — Real Cards Pass All Gates

H2.name: Value Gate Blocks — Real Cards Never Reached

H3.name: Cooldown Suppression — Real Cards, Rate Limited

H4.name: Pre-Send Gate Blocks — Real Cards + Payload Validation

H5.name: Upgrade Override — Score Improvement, Real Cards

H6.name: Full Mixed Pipeline — Real Cards, All Outcomes

key_findings: v1.11-H: Real card render + full gate pipeline dry-run complete. 26 signals across 6 scenarios. Final distribution: send_candidate=14 (53.8%), blocked_by_value=4 (15.4%), suppressed_by_cooldown=3 (11.5%), blocked_by_pre_send=5 (19.2%), observe=2 (7.7%).
Real card rendering validated: 17/19 signals used real render_card_payload(). Render success=19, render failed=0, MarkdownV2 fallback=0. Card types: {'market_anomaly': 17}.

Real card payloads successfully passed through pre_send_gate: 19 real payloads validated. This confirms that render_card_payload() output is compatible with pre_send_gate's payload validation (text presence + parse_mode field).
Zero render_card_payload() failures — all real card rendering attempts produced valid payloads. The card router handles market_anomaly signals correctly with the fields provided.
Cooldown gate suppressed 3 signal(s) — prevents same-asset spam even with valid real cards.

Pre-send gate caught 5 signal(s). Of these, some used real card payloads (blocked by source_trust/TTL) and some used mock payloads (blocked by payload_validation). This demonstrates that real cards do NOT bypass security gates.

v1.11-H confirms: the real card router is compatible with the full three-layer pipeline. Real payloads pass pre_send_gate correctly, and gate-level blocks (source_trust, TTL) work regardless of payload content.

conditions_met: Real card rendering works correctly — all real payloads produced valid output with proper structure for pre_send_gate.
Pipeline successfully identifies send_candidate signals with real card payloads — signals pass all three gates with actual TG cards.
Pre-send gate continues to block unsafe signals even with real payloads — security is not compromised by real card rendering.
blockers: This is a dry-run only. No signals were actually delivered to TG. Real delivery requires: (a) actual TG send function, (b) production config, (c) cooldown state persistence.
Formal channel remains frozen. Content value proof not yet established — cards may still be 'market noise' rather than intelligence.
recommendations: Next step (v1.11-I): Pre-send rehearsal — generate the full send list with real card payload previews, log everything, but do NOT send to TG. This is the final dry-run before test-channel delivery.
Consider adding payload text preview sampling to the handoff report — show the first 200 chars of a sample real card to verify content quality.

scenarios.scenario_name: Full Happy Path — Real Cards Pass All Gates
scenarios.objective: Verify that well-confirmed signals render real TG cards via render_card_payload(), and those real payloads pass pre_send_gate. All should be final_send_candidate.

scenarios.scenario_name: Pre-Send Gate Blocks — Real Cards + Payload Validation
scenarios.objective: Verify that pre_send_gate correctly blocks: (a) real card payload with source_type='unknown' → trust gate block, (b) real card payload with stale timestamp → TTL block, (c) mock empty payload → payload validation block, (d) mock missing parse_mode → payload validation block.

pre_send_block_reasons: AVAX: source_type 'unknown' not allowed for test send
LTC: TTL expired: signal_type='market_anomaly' TTL=900s, age=7200s
NEAR: Payload text is empty or whitespace-only

scenarios.scenario_name: Upgrade Override — Score Improvement, Real Cards
scenarios.objective: Verify upgrade_override with real card rendering. First ETH is moderate (score~55), second ETH is strong (score~100). The Δ>=15 triggers upgrade_override, and both real card payloads pass pre_send.
